_rationale: code_insight with no arguments defaults to mode=overview on both Go and C++. Go emits REPOSITORY MAP + HEALTH only. C++ enriches the overview with session-startup sections Go has no counterpart for — SUMMARY, ENTRY POINTS, DEPENDENCIES, NEXT STEPS (and VOCABULARY when the corpus has non-standard naming); added in c735c33 for AI session-startup orientation. Those C++-only sections are stripped by the text_normalize mask below so parity asserts the common REPOSITORY MAP + HEALTH subset is byte-identical on multi-lang. The integration golden (tests/integration/goldens/mcp/code_insight/basic.json) locks the FULL C++ output including the enrichment sections; this descriptor locks the Go-common subset. The mode-statistics/structure/unified/git_analyze/git_hotspots descriptors lock the per-mode dispatch path; this descriptor locks the default (no-mode-given) behaviour so a regression that changes the common subset would surface here even if every explicit mode still works.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope matches MCP spec. After the C++-only session-startup sections are masked, result.content[].text is byte-for-byte identical to Go's REPOSITORY MAP + HEALTH overview payload on multi-lang.

text_normalize._rationale_replace: Strips the C++-only session-startup sections (SUMMARY, ENTRY POINTS, DEPENDENCIES, NEXT STEPS, VOCABULARY) so the residual payload is the Go-common REPOSITORY MAP + HEALTH subset. These are intentional C++ enrichments with no Go counterpart (c735c33); Go's overview never emits them. On the flat multi-lang corpus C++ DOES emit SUMMARY/ENTRY POINTS/NEXT STEPS, so this mask is load-bearing here (not a no-op). The full enriched output is locked separately by the integration golden.
